prompt: I need to scrape apartment listings from Zillow NYC, but normal extraction isn't working. Can you use browser-use to investigate the page so we can figure out the right selectors?
response_summary: Without skill, Claude has no knowledge of the Tier 6 investigation workflow. Likely responses: (a) Says it cannot browse the web and refers to WebFetch which also fails on Zillow, (b) Tries to run WebFetch on Zillow URL and gets back only the disclaimer content, (c) Offers to help write Python code using requests/playwright without connecting it to a real investigation workflow, (d) May hallucinate plausible-sounding Zillow CSS selectors from training data (e.g. '.StyledCard', '.list-card') without any live verification. No mention of browser-use as an investigation tool. No structured escalation menu.

assertions.name: checks_nimble_first
assertions.reason: Does not know about nimble CLI — would use WebFetch or Python code instead

assertions.name: recognizes_empty_result
assertions.reason: If WebFetch returns disclaimers, likely doesn't identify this as a blocked/empty result pattern

assertions.name: checks_browser_use_availability
assertions.reason: No knowledge of browser-use as an installed tool — does not check availability

assertions.name: offers_tier6_workflow
assertions.reason: No concept of investigation → nimble command pipeline; may suggest ad-hoc Python/Playwright instead

assertions.name: does_not_hallucinate_selectors
assertions.reason: Likely guesses Zillow CSS selectors from training data (.list-card, .StyledCard, etc.) without live verification

assertions.name: presents_menu_or_offers_proceed
assertions.reason: No structured menu — responds in free-form prose with unclear next steps

notes: Without skill: no awareness of Tier 6, no nimble usage, likely hallucinated selectors or unhelpful WebFetch attempt.
